global_live_blocker: No human-provided non-production classic sandbox profile, account tier, region, authentication context, or server revision was available. Live-dependent conclusions are blocked-live-evidence.

rows.operation: discover classic Agent Server assistants and source identity

evidence.finding: Classic Agent Server exposes deployment-scoped assistants, threads, runs, state, and streams.
Pinned public package source identifies candidate langgraph-sdk 0.4.2 APIs, but public-index egress was unavailable and no installed distribution contract was captured.
unavailable

request.authentication: source-specific server-side credential; exact live header blocked

response_schema.candidate_assistant_source: list[Assistant] or AssistantsSearchResponse depending pagination mode

errors: HTTP status errors are raised by the SDK
disabled metadata can remove /info and make /ok shallow
authentication and entitlement errors require live classification
rows.idempotency: Read-only search; retry only with the same deployment-scoped filters and source identity.
rows.retry: Bounded retry for transport/5xx only; never fan out from one source result as organization-global truth.
rows.reconnect: Recreate the client with the same explicit source record; no universal auth/header inference.
rows.fallback: Keep the source unavailable and expose no assistants until a pinned classic source passes authenticated discovery.

evidence.finding: Agent Server publishes assistant read, graph, schema, subgraph, and version operations.
Pinned public package source shows candidate assistant methods, but no installed distribution contract was captured.
unavailable

request.authentication: same explicit Agent Server source context as assistant discovery

response_schema.qualification: The candidate SDK source does not narrow agent-defined input, output, config, context, or state schema fields.

errors: schema unavailable for the deployed graph
permission and version errors require live classification
rows.idempotency: Read-only for a fixed assistant/version; cache only with source and version provenance.
rows.retry: Bounded read retry; invalidate on assistant version change.
rows.reconnect: Refetch schemas after source/package/server revision changes.
rows.fallback: Treat configuration as opaque/read-only and disable config-derived form editing until exact deployed schemas are observed.

evidence.finding: The control plane is separate from Agent Server and documents /v2/deployments with X-Api-Key plus X-Tenant-Id.
Pinned SDK source is data-plane oriented and does not establish a deployment control-plane client; no installed distribution contract was captured.
unavailable
Create/update/delete states, idempotency, region behavior, and exact error bodies are not qualified for this account.

request.authentication: server-side X-Api-Key and X-Tenant-Id; values must never enter retained evidence

response_schema.documented: deployment lifecycle objects; exact accepted fields and states require package/live proof

errors: entitlement, tenant, region, build, and lifecycle errors remain unclassified without live evidence
rows.idempotency: Unknown for create/update; do not retry mutations automatically.
rows.retry: Read status only after an explicitly authorized mutation; no mutation was attempted.
rows.cancel: Unknown and not inferred from Agent Server run cancellation.
rows.reconnect: Poll only a verified deployment identity and state contract.
rows.fallback: Deployment remains an external operator-owned workflow; Deep Work may connect an already deployed classic source but must not expose deploy controls.

rows.operation: create a thread-scoped run from task input

evidence.finding: Agent Server separates thread creation and run creation; agent input/config/state schemas are deployment-defined.
Pinned SDK source shows a candidate RunsClient.create shape, but no installed distribution contract was captured.
unavailable

request.input_shape: agent-defined and must be checked against get_schemas

errors: auth and server errors require live fixtures
rows.idempotency: RunsClient.create exposes no caller run_id or mutation idempotency key. if_not_exists controls missing-thread behavior, not duplicate-run deduplication; the application must keep its own attempt record.
rows.retry: Never retry an ambiguous run-create mutation until the source is reconciled.
rows.cancel: Use the separately qualified run cancel operation.
rows.reconnect: Refetch thread/run identity before joining or subscribing.
rows.fallback: Keep submit disabled for the source and retain the task as an application draft.

rows.operation: create, read, and deployment-scoped search of threads

evidence.finding: Thread search belongs to one Agent Server deployment and is not organization-global.
Pinned SDK source shows candidate create/get/search methods with limit/offset pagination, but no installed distribution contract was captured.
unavailable

errors: source partial failure must remain source-qualified
rows.idempotency: Explicit thread_id plus if_exists controls local duplicate behavior; exact server race behavior needs live proof.
rows.retry: Reads may retry; ambiguous create requires get-by-id reconciliation.
rows.reconnect: Refresh each source independently and preserve that source's limit/offset position; the installed-source candidate does not expose a cursor.
rows.fallback: Show no runtime threads for an unqualified source; do not present deployment search as global search.

rows.operation: base run stream and resumable run join

evidence.finding: Run stream/join is distinct from protocol-v3 event subscription; resumable join uses Last-Event-ID only when stream_resumable is enabled.
Pinned SDK source shows candidate stream and join_stream parameters, but no installed distribution contract was captured.
unavailable

request.resume: Last-Event-ID applies only to qualified resumable join

errors: retention gap behavior requires live proof
rows.idempotency: Joining is read-like; starting a stream may create a run and is not automatically replay-safe.
rows.retry: Rejoin only a known run with its qualified cursor; otherwise refetch state.
rows.cancel: cancel_on_disconnect is an explicit request behavior, not a universal default.
rows.reconnect: Use last_event_id only on the resumable join contract; never reuse it for protocol v3.
rows.fallback: Poll run and thread state, then render a non-live snapshot without claiming lossless replay.

rows.operation: project stream modes and concurrent-input strategies

evidence.finding: Agent Server documents reject, interrupt, rollback, and enqueue multitask strategies; frontend submit helpers are not universal wire contracts.
Pinned SDK source types four candidate strategies, but no installed distribution or live strategy behavior was captured.
unavailable

response_schema.projection_rule: Preserve source/run/mode identity; do not flatten tool, message, update, checkpoint, or task events into one untyped payload.

errors: strategy-specific destructive or ordering behavior requires live proof
rows.idempotency: Each submitted input needs an application idempotency record; enqueue order is not inferred without live evidence.
rows.retry: Never switch strategy on retry; reconcile source state first.
rows.cancel: interrupt and rollback multitask strategies are not aliases for explicit run cancel.
rows.reconnect: Reconstruct current run/thread state before resuming projected events.
rows.fallback: Disable steering for the source; retain new text as an application draft until the active run reaches a terminal state.

evidence.finding: Beta protocol v3 subscribes through POST /threads/{thread_id}/stream/events and resumes with body field since; event_id is durable identity while seq is replay position.
Pinned SDK source contains candidate stream controller/subscription surfaces, but no installed distribution contract was captured and hosted retention remains unknown.
unavailable

request.command_separation: run.start and input.respond are separate thread command operations

response_schema.documented: SSE ProtocolEvent with type, seq, method, params.namespace, params.timestamp, params.data, and optional durable event_id
response_schema.cursor_rule: Persist source, thread, run, protocol, seq, and event_id.

errors: reauthentication and cancellation races require live proof
rows.idempotency: Deduplicate user-visible projection by durable event_id within source/run identity.
rows.retry: Reconnect with since equal to the last confirmed seq only; refetch state on a gap.
rows.cancel: Subscription cancellation does not prove run cancellation.
rows.reconnect: Never send Last-Event-ID for protocol v3; use body field since.
rows.fallback: Use qualified legacy run join if separately accepted; otherwise refetch thread state and show non-live recovery.

rows.operation: observe and respond to ordered human-in-the-loop interrupts

evidence.finding: Deep Agents HITL aligns action_requests and review_configs and requires one ordered decision per action; documented decisions are approve, edit, reject, and respond subject to config.
Pinned package sources expose candidate Interrupt and Command shapes; no installed distribution contract was captured and the interrupt value remains agent-defined.
unavailable

request.ordering: Exactly one decision per action request, in source order.
request.wire: The exact hosted input.respond/run resume mapping requires live proof.

response_schema.candidate_package_source: ThreadState.interrupts: list[Interrupt] where Interrupt has id and open value

rows.idempotency: Persist an application decision attempt; on ambiguity refetch pending state before any retry.
rows.retry: Never resend solely because the response was lost; reject a stale duplicate.
rows.cancel: Cancellation is separately qualified and must not synthesize an approval decision.
rows.reconnect: Refetch current interrupts, preserve source order, and reconcile the attempted decision.
rows.fallback: Render the interrupt read-only and disable submit; do not flatten, force-resolve, or invent ignore.

evidence.finding: Agent Server exposes run cancel and distinguishes interrupt from rollback actions.
Pinned SDK source shows a candidate cancel signature with interrupt or rollback, but no installed distribution contract was captured.
unavailable

response_schema.qualification: A successful return does not by itself establish terminal source state; refetch run/thread.

errors: run not found or already terminal
rows.idempotency: Not established; reconcile run state before repeating.
rows.retry: Do not retry an ambiguous cancellation until run status is refetched.
rows.cancel: interrupt preserves prior checkpoints; rollback is destructive and must require explicit authority.
rows.reconnect: After disconnect, refetch run and thread state and classify cancellation outcome.
rows.fallback: Disable cancel and show the run as source-controlled; allow refresh only.

rows.operation: read thread state and checkpoint history

evidence.finding: Agent Server exposes current state, checkpoint-qualified state, history, and state update operations.
Pinned SDK source shows candidate state/history parameters, but no installed distribution contract was captured.
unavailable

errors: history ordering and retention require live proof
rows.idempotency: Reads are safe for a fixed source/thread/checkpoint identity; state update is a separate mutation and is not qualified here.
rows.retry: Bounded read retry; surface pruned/unavailable rather than substituting the latest checkpoint.
rows.cancel: Checkpoint read does not cancel or roll back a run.
rows.reconnect: Refetch current state before replaying transient stream effects.
rows.fallback: Show current state only when available and disable checkpoint restore/branch actions.
